stepBody: A researcher considers the following scheme for splitting people into control and treatment groups. People are arranged in a line and for each person, a fair, six-sided die is rolled. If the die comes up to be a $$1$$ or a $$2$$, the person is allocated to the treatment group. If the die comes up to be a $$3$$, $$4$$, $$5$$, or $$6$$ then the person is allocated to the control group. This is a randomized control experiment.
